Name: quantization_tradeoffs
Title: Understanding Quantization Trade-offs in Model Compression through Rate-Distortion Theory
Short Hypothesis: The trade-off between quantization precision and task-relevant information can be framed as a rate-distortion problem, revealing optimal quantization strategies necessary for preserving predictive sufficiency in compressed models.
Related Work: While prior work has explored model compression through lossy compression and generalization bounds, there is limited research explicitly framing quantization strategies as a rate-distortion problem focused on task-relevant information. Existing literature has primarily addressed compression without dissecting the specific trade-offs of quantization methods regarding predictive performance, thus leaving a gap in understanding how to optimize quantization within the context of model compression.
Abstract: This proposal investigates the quantization strategies in model compression through the lens of rate-distortion theory, aiming to uncover the optimal trade-offs between quantization precision and task-relevant information preservation. By framing quantization as a lossy source coding problem, we propose to derive operational bounds on the bits-per-weight necessary to maintain predictive sufficiency in compressed models. We will conduct experiments to evaluate various quantization schemes, measuring their impact on predictive performance relative to theoretical information-theoretic limits. The insights gained will not only enhance our understanding of the quantization process in model compression but will also provide a unified framework for future compression techniques that prioritize task relevance over merely minimizing model size. This work seeks to bridge the gap between empirical findings and principled theoretical foundations, setting the stage for more sophisticated and informed compression strategies.
Experiments: 1. Implement various quantization strategies (uniform, non-uniform, and learned quantization) on a benchmark model and dataset. Measure the predictive performance (accuracy) of the compressed models against theoretical rate-distortion bounds.

2. Analyze the relationship between the quantization levels and the mutual information retained between the model's output and the task-specific labels. This will involve estimating the mutual information before and after applying quantization.

3. Perform a sensitivity analysis on the model's performance concerning different distortion measures (e.g., mean squared error, cross-entropy) to determine which measures yield better performance for specific tasks.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The main risks include potential difficulties in estimating mutual information accurately, which may lead to challenges in validating theoretical predictions. Furthermore, the choice of distortion measure could impact the results, and the findings may not generalize across all types of models or datasets.
